input.prompt:
Review pull request #10 on the dtran320/coderegon-trail repository.

This PR adds 10 new Coderegon Trail games (Oregon Trail-style educational games) sourced from top Show HN posts, plus integrates DeepWiki MCP into the trail-data-extractor agent.

Key files to review:
- `agents/trail-data-extractor.md` — DeepWiki integration (new Step 0 for repo analysis)
- `index.html` — 10 new entries in TRAILS array + fallback links
- `scripts/generate-game.sh` — New game generation template script
- `*/index.html` — 10 new game files (kittentts, craftplan, localgpt, pg-textsearch, moongate, ferrite, gotreesitter, mini-browser, korb, cmux)
- `*/trail-data.json` — Trail data for each game
- `*/customizations.js` — Per-game customizations (title, travel flavors, event overlays)

Focus on:
1. Whether the game HTML files will work correctly (proper JS structure, no missing variables)
2. Whether the index.html updates are consistent
3. Whether the trail-data-extractor agent changes are sound
4. Any security concerns in the generate-game.sh script

Get the PR diff with: `gh pr diff 10`
Get file contents with: Read tool on the local files (they're in the current working directory)

Report findings with confidence levels.

content.text:
Good — only cmux has the stray `/`. Now let me summarize my full findings.

---

## Review of PR #10: HN Popular Trails + DeepWiki Integration

Reviewing the diff for the `feature/shared-engine` branch, focusing on the 10 new game files, the generate-game.sh script, index.html updates, and agent changes.

---

## Critical Issues

### 1. `generate-game.sh` omits the `drawEventOverlay` function declaration — 8 games are broken (Confidence: 100)

**Files:** `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/scripts/generate-game.sh` lines 54-63; affects kittentts, craftplan, localgpt, moongate, korb, cmux, gotreesitter, mini-browser

The script assembles the game engine by splicing sections from the openclaw template with this structure:

```
sed -n '206,489p'   # shared engine — stops at line 489 (the closing comment line)
$OVERLAYS           # custom overlay code from customizations.js
echo "}"            # closes the function
sed -n '711,$p'     # rest of engine
```

The template's `drawEventOverlay` function declaration is at **line 490**. The script slices lines 206–489, then immediately inserts the custom overlay body, then emits `}`. This means the generated files contain overlay code with no enclosing `function drawEventOverlay(time) {` declaration. The function body appears at the top level of the script block, causes a parse error on the stray `}`, and `drawEventOverlay` is never defined — so the game engine's call to it at the animation loop throws a `ReferenceError` on every frame.

Cross-checking confirms: none of kittentts, craftplan, localgpt, moongate, korb, cmux, gotreesitter, or mini-browser's `index.html` files contain `function drawEventOverlay`. All 8 games will fail to render event overlays and produce JavaScript errors.

The two games that work (ferrite and pg-textsearch) escaped the bug by manually including `function drawEventOverlay(time) {` as the first line of their `// OVERLAYS` section in `customizations.js` — an inconsistency that reveals the script's design expectation is broken.

**Fix:** Change line 54 of `generate-game.sh` from `sed -n '206,489p'` to `sed -n '206,490p'` (include the function declaration line), and remove the `echo "}"` on line 61 (it's already present in the template as the closing brace at line 709). Or, more robustly, output lines 206–709 from the template minus lines 189–205 (the openclaw-specific travelFlavors) and the OpenClaw overlay body (lines 496–708), replacing just those lines with the custom content.

---

### 2. `cmux/customizations.js` line 24 contains a bare `/` operator where a comment was intended (Confidence: 100)

**File:** `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/cmux/customizations.js` line 24; propagated to `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/cmux/index.html` line 732

The line reads:
```
  / --- CONFIG BLIZZARD: snow and cold wind ---
```

A JS comment requires `//` or `/* */`. A bare `/` is a division operator. In the context where it appears (inside the function body, after the `if (!currentEventType && !currentEventTitle) return;` guard), the JavaScript engine will attempt to parse `/ --- CONFIG BLIZZARD: snow and cold wind ---` as a regex literal, and fail on the unterminated regex, throwing a `SyntaxError` that kills the entire script block. The cmux game will not load at all.

**Fix:** Change `/ ---` to `// ---` on line 24 of `cmux/customizations.js` and regenerate `cmux/index.html`.

---

### 3. `pg-textsearch/customizations.js` TITLE section contains a JS object literal, not a title string (Confidence: 100)

**File:** `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/pg-textsearch/customizations.js` lines 1–19; propagated to `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/pg-textsearch/index.html` lines 6–21

The `// TITLE` ... `// END TITLE` block contains a `TITLE_CONFIG` JavaScript object literal rather than a plain text string. The `generate-game.sh` script extracts this block and inserts it verbatim into `<title>$TITLE</title>`, producing:

```html
<title>const TITLE_CONFIG = {
  title: "THE RELEVANCE TRAIL",
  ...
};</title>
```

This is confirmed in the generated `pg-textsearch/index.html` lines 6–21. The browser's tab title will show raw JavaScript source code. It also means the `TITLE_CONFIG` JS object is never actually defined in the script context (it only appears in the `<title>` tag), so any JS code that references it will throw a `ReferenceError`.

**Fix:** Replace the `// TITLE` block in `pg-textsearch/customizations.js` with a single plain-text line like `The Coderegon Trail - pg_textsearch Edition`, and regenerate the file.

---

## Important Issues

### 4. `gotreesitter/customizations.js` and `mini-browser/customizations.js` don't use the `// TITLE` / `// FLAVORS` / `// OVERLAYS` section markers expected by `generate-game.sh` (Confidence: 100)

**Files:** `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/gotreesitter/customizations.js`, `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/mini-browser/customizations.js`

`gotreesitter/customizations.js` uses `document.title = ...`, `window.TRAIL_FLAVORS = [...]`, and `window.drawCustomEventOverlay = function(...)`. `mini-browser/customizations.js` uses `const GAME_TITLE`, `const GAME_SUBTITLE`, and `const travelFlavors` without the section markers.

When `generate-game.sh` processes these files, the `sed` extraction of TITLE/FLAVORS/OVERLAYS sections produces empty strings. The resulting games have:
- An empty `<title></title>` tag
- The openclaw `travelFlavors` retained (lines 189–205 of the template are NOT overridden — they're outside the 206–489 range)

Wait — actually lines 189–205 are not included in the `sed -n '206,489p'` range, so the generated file won't have any `travelFlavors` at all. The `travelFlavorIndex` variable references an empty or undefined array. Looking at the gotreesitter and mini-browser generated files, their `travelFlavors` arrays are present at lines 499 and 514 respectively — which suggests these files were NOT generated by the script but were hand-built (which explains why they have content). content.text:
Good — only cmux has the stray `/`. Now let me summarize my full findings.

---

## Review of PR #10: HN Popular Trails + DeepWiki Integration

Reviewing the diff for the `feature/shared-engine` branch, focusing on the 10 new game files, the generate-game.sh script, index.html updates, and agent changes.

---

## Critical Issues

### 1. `generate-game.sh` omits the `drawEventOverlay` function declaration — 8 games are broken (Confidence: 100)

**Files:** `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/scripts/generate-game.sh` lines 54-63; affects kittentts, craftplan, localgpt, moongate, korb, cmux, gotreesitter, mini-browser

The script assembles the game engine by splicing sections from the openclaw template with this structure:

```
sed -n '206,489p'   # shared engine — stops at line 489 (the closing comment line)
$OVERLAYS           # custom overlay code from customizations.js
echo "}"            # closes the function
sed -n '711,$p'     # rest of engine
```

The template's `drawEventOverlay` function declaration is at **line 490**. The script slices lines 206–489, then immediately inserts the custom overlay body, then emits `}`. This means the generated files contain overlay code with no enclosing `function drawEventOverlay(time) {` declaration. The function body appears at the top level of the script block, causes a parse error on the stray `}`, and `drawEventOverlay` is never defined — so the game engine's call to it at the animation loop throws a `ReferenceError` on every frame.

Cross-checking confirms: none of kittentts, craftplan, localgpt, moongate, korb, cmux, gotreesitter, or mini-browser's `index.html` files contain `function drawEventOverlay`. All 8 games will fail to render event overlays and produce JavaScript errors.

The two games that work (ferrite and pg-textsearch) escaped the bug by manually including `function drawEventOverlay(time) {` as the first line of their `// OVERLAYS` section in `customizations.js` — an inconsistency that reveals the script's design expectation is broken.

**Fix:** Change line 54 of `generate-game.sh` from `sed -n '206,489p'` to `sed -n '206,490p'` (include the function declaration line), and remove the `echo "}"` on line 61 (it's already present in the template as the closing brace at line 709). Or, more robustly, output lines 206–709 from the template minus lines 189–205 (the openclaw-specific travelFlavors) and the OpenClaw overlay body (lines 496–708), replacing just those lines with the custom content.

---

### 2. `cmux/customizations.js` line 24 contains a bare `/` operator where a comment was intended (Confidence: 100)

**File:** `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/cmux/customizations.js` line 24; propagated to `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/cmux/index.html` line 732

The line reads:
```
  / --- CONFIG BLIZZARD: snow and cold wind ---
```

A JS comment requires `//` or `/* */`. A bare `/` is a division operator. In the context where it appears (inside the function body, after the `if (!currentEventType && !currentEventTitle) return;` guard), the JavaScript engine will attempt to parse `/ --- CONFIG BLIZZARD: snow and cold wind ---` as a regex literal, and fail on the unterminated regex, throwing a `SyntaxError` that kills the entire script block. The cmux game will not load at all.

**Fix:** Change `/ ---` to `// ---` on line 24 of `cmux/customizations.js` and regenerate `cmux/index.html`.

---

### 3. `pg-textsearch/customizations.js` TITLE section contains a JS object literal, not a title string (Confidence: 100)

**File:** `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/pg-textsearch/customizations.js` lines 1–19; propagated to `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/pg-textsearch/index.html` lines 6–21

The `// TITLE` ... `// END TITLE` block contains a `TITLE_CONFIG` JavaScript object literal rather than a plain text string. The `generate-game.sh` script extracts this block and inserts it verbatim into `<title>$TITLE</title>`, producing:

```html
<title>const TITLE_CONFIG = {
  title: "THE RELEVANCE TRAIL",
  ...
};</title>
```

This is confirmed in the generated `pg-textsearch/index.html` lines 6–21. The browser's tab title will show raw JavaScript source code. It also means the `TITLE_CONFIG` JS object is never actually defined in the script context (it only appears in the `<title>` tag), so any JS code that references it will throw a `ReferenceError`.

**Fix:** Replace the `// TITLE` block in `pg-textsearch/customizations.js` with a single plain-text line like `The Coderegon Trail - pg_textsearch Edition`, and regenerate the file.

---

## Important Issues

### 4. `gotreesitter/customizations.js` and `mini-browser/customizations.js` don't use the `// TITLE` / `// FLAVORS` / `// OVERLAYS` section markers expected by `generate-game.sh` (Confidence: 100)

**Files:** `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/gotreesitter/customizations.js`, `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/mini-browser/customizations.js`

`gotreesitter/customizations.js` uses `document.title = ...`, `window.TRAIL_FLAVORS = [...]`, and `window.drawCustomEventOverlay = function(...)`. `mini-browser/customizations.js` uses `const GAME_TITLE`, `const GAME_SUBTITLE`, and `const travelFlavors` without the section markers.

When `generate-game.sh` processes these files, the `sed` extraction of TITLE/FLAVORS/OVERLAYS sections produces empty strings. The resulting games have:
- An empty `<title></title>` tag
- The openclaw `travelFlavors` retained (lines 189–205 of the template are NOT overridden — they're outside the 206–489 range)

Wait — actually lines 189–205 are not included in the `sed -n '206,489p'` range, so the generated file won't have any `travelFlavors` at all. The `travelFlavorIndex` variable references an empty or undefined array. Looking at the gotreesitter and mini-browser generated files, their `travelFlavors` arrays are present at lines 499 and 514 respectively — which suggests these files were NOT generated by the script but were hand-built (which explains why they have content). However, the customizations.js files are present in the repo seemingly to enable future regeneration but would produce broken output if regenerated.

This is a documentation/maintainability gap: the customizations.js files for gotreesitter and mini-browser are not compatible with generate-game.sh, and would produce broken output if the script were re-run on them. The generate-game.sh script has no validation or error output when sections are missing.

**Fix:** Either rewrite gotreesitter/customizations.js and mini-browser/customizations.js to use the `// TITLE` / `// FLAVORS` / `// OVERLAYS` section format, or add validation to the script that fails loudly when extracted sections are empty.

---

### 5. `korb/customizations.js` OVERLAYS section duplicates the early-return guard that already exists in the template (Confidence: 80)

**File:** `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/korb/customizations.js` lines 24–26

```js
  if (!currentEventType && !currentEventTitle) return;
  var t = (time || 0) * 0.001;
```

These two lines are already present in the template as lines 491–493. Since the script omits the function declaration (issue #1), in the broken state these lines appear at the top level causing a bare `return;` outside any function — a syntax error. Even when issue #1 is fixed (the function declaration is restored), these duplicate lines are harmless but redundant boilerplate.

However, moongate/customizations.js has the same duplicate guard lines (lines 24–26). They will cause no bugs once issue #1 is fixed, since JavaScript doesn't care about duplicate `if` checks, but they indicate the customizations.js authors were uncertain about what the template already provides.

---

## Agent Changes (agents/trail-data-extractor.md)

The DeepWiki pre-analysis step (Step 0) is correctly marked as optional and best-effort. The `Bash(curl:*)` tool is already in the agent's frontmatter. The `| head -500` safety limit on the full wiki fetch is appropriate. The step is well-integrated and doesn't change any existing behavior — it's purely additive. No issues found.

---

## index.html Updates

The TRAILS array (23 entries) and fallback links (22 `<a>` tags) are internally consistent. All 10 new trail names, subtitles, hrefs, and colors match between the TRAILS array and the fallback list. The hardcoded `DISK 1 OF 23` in the static HUD is overridden by `updateHUD()` at runtime, so it's not a bug. No issues found here.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Generate a game HTML file from a trail data JSON file + customizations
# Usage: ./scripts/generate-game.sh <game-dir> <trail-data.json> <customizations.js>
#
# trail-data.json: The TRAIL_DATA JSON object
# customizations.js: File containing:
#   - TITLE: single line with the <title> text
#   - FLAVORS: the travelFlavors array (JS)
#   - OVERLAYS: the drawEventOverlay function body (JS)

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
TEMPLATE="$PROJECT_ROOT/openclaw/index.html"

GAME_DIR="${1:?Usage: generate-game.sh <game-dir> <trail-data.json> <customizations.js>}"
TRAIL_DATA_FILE="${2:?Missing trail-data.json}"
CUSTOMIZATIONS_FILE="${3:?Missing customizations.js}"

# Create game directory
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/$GAME_DIR"

OUTPUT="$PROJECT_ROOT/$GAME_DIR/index.html"

# Read customizations file sections
TITLE=$(sed -n '/^\/\/ TITLE$/,/^\/\/ END TITLE$/{ /^\/\//d; p; }' "$CUSTOMIZATIONS_FILE")
FLAVORS=$(sed -n '/^\/\/ FLAVORS$/,/^\/\/ END FLAVORS$/{ /^\/\/ \(END \)\?FLAVORS$/d; p; }' "$CUSTOMIZATIONS_FILE")
OVERLAYS=$(sed -n '/^\/\/ OVERLAYS$/,/^\/\/ END OVERLAYS$/{ /^\/\/ \(END \)\?OVERLAYS$/d; p; }' "$CUSTOMIZATIONS_FILE")

# Read trail data
TRAIL_DATA=$(cat "$TRAIL_DATA_FILE")

# Build the game HTML by extracting template sections and inserting custom parts
{
  # Lines 1-5: HTML header before title
  sed -n '1,5p' "$TEMPLATE"

  # Custom title
  echo "<title>$TITLE</title>"

  # Lines 7-137: CSS and HTML structure after title
  sed -n '7,137p' "$TEMPLATE"

  # Trail data line
  echo "const TRAIL_DATA=$TRAIL_DATA;"

  # Lines 139-188: Game state (identical across games)
  sed -n '139,188p' "$TEMPLATE"

  # Custom travel flavors
  echo "$FLAVORS"

  # Lines 206-489: Shared engine (canvas, rendering, etc.) up to overlay function header
  sed -n '206,489p' "$TEMPLATE"

  # Custom event overlays (inside drawEventOverlay function)
  echo "$OVERLAYS"

  # Close drawEventOverlay function + rest of engine
  echo "}"
  echo ""
  sed -n '711,$p' "$TEMPLATE"

} > "$OUTPUT"

echo "Generated $OUTPUT ($(wc -c < "$OUTPUT" | tr -d ' ') bytes)"

Both files validate correctly. Let me verify the overlay count matches -- we have 6 overlays for 6 non-fortune events (the fortune event "Backout Lambda Bonus!" correctly has no overlay, as per the instructions to skip fortune events). The 6 overlay blocks match the 6 non-fortune events exactly.

Let me also verify the difficulty distribution meets the spec (2-3 easy, 2-3 medium, 1-2 hard):

- Easy: Vocabulary Blizzard (1), Backout Lambda Bonus (5) = 2 easy
- Medium: Data Wrangler (2), Embedding Dimension Rapids (3), Gradient Underflow Storm (6) = 3 medium
- Hard: Sliding Window Ambush (4), Calculator Tool Misfire (7) = 2 hard

That's 2 easy, 3 medium, 2 hard -- within spec.

**Files created:**

---

**Two files created in `/Users/flowclub/code/coderegon-trail/.claude/worktrees/hn-popular/minidiffusion/`:**

### trail-data.json
- **Framework:** minidiffusion (Stable Diffusion 3.5 reimplementation in pure PyTorch)
- **Trail Name:** "The Diffusion Trail"
- **8 stops** mapping the full diffusion pipeline:
  1. Tokenizer Trading Post (town) - BPE + Unigram text encoding from `tokenizer.py`
  2. CLIP Encoding Camp (camp) - Triple text encoder fusion from `dit_components.py`
  3. VAE Encoder Gorge (mountain) - Image compression to latent space from `vae.py`
  4. Noise Schedule River (river) - Logit-normal sampling and forward diffusion from `noise.py`
  5. Patch Embed Crossing (forest) - 2D sincos positional embeddings from `dit_components.py`
  6. Joint Attention Summit (mountain) - Paged KV cache cross-modal attention from `attention.py`
  7. DiT Block Pass (forest) - MMDiT forward pass with 24 transformer blocks from `dit.py`
  8. Generation Frontier (town) - Euler ODE solver + VAE decoding from `inference.py`
- **7 events:** 3 easy, 2 medium, 2 hard; types: 2 weather, 2 encounter, 1 river, 1 misfortune, 1 fortune
- **4 party members:** VAE Encoder, DiT Backbone, Noise Scheduler, CLIP Embeddings
- **10 death messages** themed around diffusion model failures
- All code snippets are **real code** from the yousef-rafat/miniDiffusion repo, 15-25 lines each

### customizations.js
- **Title:** "The Coderegon Trail - miniDiffusion Edition"
- **15 travel flavors** themed around diffusion model concepts (VAE, DiT, CLIP, noise scheduling, etc.)
- **6 pixel art overlays** (one per non-fortune event), each using 320x200 canvas with simple shapes:
  - Unicode Byte Storm: falling byte characters with lightning
  - Prompt Engineer: NPC with triple encoder diagram
  - Latent Space Rapids: river with floating mean/logvar vectors
  - Sigma Schedule Collapse: logit-normal vs uniform distribution curve
  - Position Embedding Fog: patch grid emerging from fog
  - Diffusion Researcher: final block architecture comparison diagram</result>

- miniDiffusion (yousef-rafat/miniDiffusion): The Diffusion Trail —

  scheduling, joint attention, DiT blocks, to Euler ODE sampling

Total trails: 25 (6 canonical + 19 external repos)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text: Yes, all 25 are in the main screen (both the 3D disk selector and fallback list). Let me look at how the 3D selector and HUD work to design category filtering.

file.content:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<title>The Coderegon Trail</title>
<script type="importmap">
{
  "imports": {
    "three": "https://esm.sh/three@0.171.0"
  }
}
</script>
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Space+Mono:wght@400;700&family=Silkscreen:wght@400;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
<style>
*, *::before, *::after { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }
html, body { height: 100%; overflow: hidden; background: #060608; }
canvas#scene { position: fixed; inset: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; display: block; }

/* ── Disk Info HUD ── */
#hud {
  position: fixed; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; z-index: 10;
  pointer-events: none; display: flex; justify-content: center;
  padding: 0 24px 32px;
}
#hud-inner {
  pointer-events: auto;
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(20,18,14,0) 0%, rgba(20,18,14,0.88) 30%, rgba(20,18,14,0.96) 100%);
  backdrop-filter: blur(8px);
  padding: 48px 48px 32px; max-width: 640px; width: 100%; text-align: center;
  border-radius: 12px 12px 0 0;
}
#hud .disk-number {
  font: 700 11px/1 'Space Mono', monospace;
  letter-spacing: 3px; text-transform: uppercase;
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.3); margin-bottom: 10px;
}
#hud .trail-name {
  font: 700 28px/1.15 'Silkscreen', monospace;
  color: #fff; margin-bottom: 6px;
  transition: color 0.4s;
}
#hud .trail-sub {
  font: 400 15px/1.4 'Space Mono', monospace;
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.5); margin-bottom: 20px;
}
#hud .controls {
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 24px;
}
#hud .nav-btn {
  background: none; border: 2px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15);
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.5); font: 700 18px/1 'Space Mono', monospace;
  width: 44px; height: 44px; border-radius: 50%; cursor: pointer;
  transition: all 0.2s; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
}
#hud .nav-btn:hover { border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.4); color: #fff; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05); }
#hud .play-btn {
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1); border: 2px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.25);
  color: #fff; font: 700 13px/1 'Space Mono', monospace;
  padding: 12px 32px; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer;
  letter-spacing: 2px; text-transform: uppercase;
  transition: all 0.2s;
}
#hud .play-btn:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.2); border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.5); }
#hud .dots {
  display: flex; gap: 6px; justify-content: center; margin-top: 16px;
}
#hud .dot {
  width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15); transition: all 0.3s;
}
#hud .dot.active { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); transform: scale(1.3); }

/* ── Fallback ── */
#fallback {
  display: none; height: 100%; overflow-y: auto;
  background: #060608; color: #e2e8f0; padding: 60px 20px;
  font-family: 'Space Mono','Courier New',monospace;
  flex-direction: column; align-items: center;
}
#fallback h1 { font: 700 28px 'Silkscreen',monospace; letter-spacing: 4px; color: #FFFF55; text-align: center; margin-bottom: 8px; }
#fallback .subtitle { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.4); font-size: 14px; text-align: center; margin-bottom: 40px; }
#fallback .trails { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 10px; width: 100%; max-width: 560px; margin: 0 auto; }
#fallback .tc { display: block; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 6px; padding: 16px 20px; text-decoration: none; color: #e2e8f0; transition: all .2s; }
#fallback .tc:hover { border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.2); background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); }
#fallback .tc .n { font-size: 15px; font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 2px; }
#fallback .tc .d { font-size: 12px; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.4); }
</style>
</head>

content.text: Good question. 25 disks in a flat carousel is a lot. Let me add category filter tabs to both the 3D selector and fallback. I'll group them as: **All** | **Frameworks** | **HN Popular** | **Community**.

Add category filter tabs to trail selector

Trails are now organized into three categories with filter pills
in both the 3D disk carousel and HTML fallback:
- Frameworks (5): Rails, Django, Express, React, Laravel
- HN Popular (10): top Show HN repos by weighted score
- Community (10): gstack, OpenClaw, nanochat, miniDiffusion, etc.

The 3D carousel dynamically rebuilds when switching categories.
The fallback view groups trails by category with section headers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push

content.text:
Done. The server is running at **http://localhost:8080** — you can test it now.

The main screen now has filter tabs: **All** | **Frameworks** | **HN Popular** | **Community**

- Clicking a tab filters the 3D disk carousel to just that category
- The fallback HTML view also has the same tabs, and in "All" mode shows section headers grouping trails by category
- The disk count updates dynamically (e.g., "DISK 1 OF 10" when filtered to HN Popular)
